commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A maintainer should decide the public env, persistence, and security boundary before any repair lane tries to finalize the PR.; Needs attention: No supply-chain changes were found, but the new persisted child-env API needs an explicit secret-handling and execution-boundary contract before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Thread env through queue session parsers — src/types.ts:220 - [P2] Warn callers that session... (sha=8769be08caecf3d6c1579963c4f5f1842bb80358)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `SessionAgentOptions.env`, passes it into agent child-process spawn with acpx auth-env protection, persists/restores `session_options.env`, and adds regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch-level blocker: a queued submit request with `sessionOptions.env` would pass the new public type but the queue parsers only copy the four pre-existing fields, so env is dropped before AcpClient creation. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should decide the public env, persistence, and security boundary before any repair lane tries to finalize the PR.

target.title: feat(session): add per-agent env to SessionAgentOptions and child spawn
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fix classification: root-cause fix at the child-spawn env merge owner.
- Root cause: `buildAgentEnvironment` injected auth credential env first, then applied `SessionAgentOptions.env` afterward with unconditional assignment, so session env with the same `ACPX_AUTH_*` or normalized auth key replaced the acpx-owned credential at the source child-spawn env merge point.
- Adds per-agent session environment injection while preserving acpx-managed auth credential variables.
- Prevents `SessionAgentOptions.env` from replacing injected auth credentials such as `ACPX_AUTH_API_TOKEN` or the normalized `API_TOKEN` form, including differently-cased session env keys on Windows.
- Preserves persisted `session_options.env` through disk parsing, conversation-state cloning, and `startTurn` reconnect client creation.

Why does this matter now?

- The original per-agent env support intentionally lets callers shadow normal parent-process values, but auth credentials are acpx-owned secrets and should not be silently downgraded by session env input.

What is the intended outcome?

- Why this fixes the root cause: the protected-key set is built at the same source-of-truth boundary that owns auth credential env injection, before session env is merged, so the later generic session-env merge cannot overwrite acpx-owned credential keys.
- Callers can still override ordinary child-process env keys like `GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL` per agent.
- Auth credential env keys generated or promoted by acpx keep the credential value even when session env contains the same key; on Windows the protected-key comparison follows environment key case-insensitivity.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- What did NOT change: no new env key denylist beyond acpx-managed auth credential keys.
- Not changed: no change to auth credential lookup order or how credentials are selected for ACP authentication.
- Public contract surface: `SessionAgentOptions.env` remains a per-agent child-process env override for ordinary keys; the canonical contract boundary is that acpx-owned auth credential keys are not caller-overridable through this generic env map.
- Compatibility/default behavior: ordinary partial override behavior is backward-compatible for non-auth env keys; only attempts to override acpx-managed auth credentials are intentionally blocked.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: this is the existing PR #389 update; no separate acpx issue is linked, and the only related references are cross-repository OpenClaw context that could not be fetched from this repository.
- No schema, dependency, migration, protocol, or wire-format changes beyond the existing optional session env field.

What does success look like?

- Session env reaches spawned agents for regular variables.
- Auth credentials injected by acpx remain protected in both `ACPX_AUTH_*` and normalized key forms.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `src/acp/auth-env.ts` is the owner of auth credential env names and child-spawn env assembly, so the protection lives there rather than in downstream callers or tests.
- Whether the protected-key boundary matches the auth env keys acpx owns: explicitly configured auth credentials and promoted `ACPX_AUTH_*` parent env values, with Windows key comparison handled case-insensitively.
- Patch quality warning explanation: the lower-priority option wording in the session-options diff is existing merge vocabulary for persisted/default options; it is separate from the auth fix, which protects acpx-owned credential keys at the source env merge boundary.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

None. This updates existing PR #389; no acpx issue is closed by this PR.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related openclaw/openclaw#93425 and openclaw/openclaw#93752, as noted in the original PR body.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- This updates the existing author PR after identifying an auth credential override risk in the per-agent env implementation.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `buildAgentSpawnOptions` merges per-agent session env into the real child-process environment while protecting acpx-injected auth env credentials, and persisted session env is restored on reconnect and model preference updates.
- Real environment tested: Node test build, child-process spawn behavior, session persistence parsing, runtime reconnect behavior, and model preference clearing behavior on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" run build:test && node --test "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/spawn-options.test.js" "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/session-persistence.test.js" "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/runtime-manager.test.js" "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/session-mode-preference.test.js"`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Validation `post-commit-env-protection-persistence-reconnect-model-clear` passed with exit code 0:

  ```text
  > acpx@0.10.0 build:test [local path redacted]
  > node -e "require('node:fs').rmSync('dist-test',{recursive:true,force:true})" && tsgo -p tsconfig.test.json

  ✔ AcpRuntimeManager restores persisted session env when reconnecting startTurn
  ✔ setDesiredModelId preserves session env when clearing model
  ✔ persistSessionOptions preserves session env as a serialized record
  ✔ sessionOptionsFromRecord restores session env from a persisted record
  ✔ parseSessionRecord preserves persisted session env
  ✔ buildAgentSpawnOptions prevents session env from overriding injected auth env
  ✔ buildAgentSpawnOptions protects auth env case-insensitively on Windows
  ✔ buildAgentSpawnOptions protects inherited auth env case-insensitively on Windows
  ✔ buildAgentSpawnOptions promotes explicit ACPX auth env vars into agent auth env
  ℹ tests 95
  ℹ pass 95
  ℹ fail 0
  ```

- Observed result after fix: session env still reaches spawned child processes for normal variables; persisted `session_options.env` survives parsing from disk, reconnect state cloning, and desired-model clearing; `startTurn` reconnect passes restored session env back into client creation; and the regressions confirm prefixed, normalized, Windows differently-cased, and inherited `acpx_auth_*` auth env keys keep the acpx-owned credential instead of the session-provided value.
- What was not tested: a full OpenClaw gateway run against the downstream OpenClaw integration was not run for this acpx library change.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the linked OpenClaw issue/PR references are cross-repository context; the local proof focuses on the acpx child-spawn contract that this PR changes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new regression failed before the fix with `actual 'session-prefixed'` instead of expected `secret-token` for `ACPX_AUTH_API_TOKEN`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm -C "$TASK_WORKTREE" run build:test && node --test "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/spawn-options.test.js" "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/session-persistence.test.js" "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/runtime-manager.test.js" "$TASK_WORKTREE/dist-test/test/session-mode-preference.test.js"` — passed, 95 tests.
- ClawSweeper commit review — passed, nothing found.
- Pre-commit hook during commit — passed `oxfmt --write`, `oxlint --fix`, and `pnpm run -s build`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added focused regressions in `test/spawn-options.test.ts` proving session env cannot override prefixed, normalized, Windows differently-cased, or inherited lowercase auth credential env keys.
- Added a parser-level regression in `test/session-persistence.test.ts` proving persisted `session_options.env` is restored from disk and non-string env values are ignored.
- Added a runtime-manager regression proving persisted session env is restored when `startTurn` reconnects to an existing session record.
- Added a mode-preference regression proving clearing a desired model keeps persisted `session_options.env`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The new regression failed because `sessionEnv` was applied after auth credential injection and replaced `ACPX_AUTH_API_TOKEN` with the caller-provided session value.

If no test was added, why not?

- A regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — per-agent session env no longer overrides acpx-managed auth credential env keys.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — auth credential env handling is now protected from session env override.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Existing callers that attempted to use session env to override `ACPX_AUTH_*` or normalized auth credential keys, including different casing on Windows, will no longer be able to replace those protected values through `SessionAgentOptions.env`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The restriction is scoped to auth credential keys acpx manages; ordinary session env override behavior remains unchanged for non-auth keys.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review after the existing PR branch is updated.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer review and remote CI/check status after push.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- No actionable reviewer or bot comments were detected; this update addresses the auth credential override risk found during follow-up review.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
